pi_items.source: ImageJ Fiji documentation confirms Mitosis sample has 51 time frames. Multiple sources verify this dimension: https://imagejdocu.list.lu/gui/file/open_samples and https://imagej.net/plugins/samples

Kymograph generation process: reslicing a time-lapse stack (51 frames) along the temporal axis produces height = number of time frames. Task description and verifier.py (line 58-64) confirm this expectation.

This is a task-specific constraint on the minimum width of the straight line to be drawn across the dividing cell. Not independently verifiable without cell size specifications. Verifier.py (line 67-71) enforces this as a practical requirement.
Path specified in task.json description, setup_task.sh, and export_result.sh. Consistent across all task files.
Path specified in task.json description, setup_task.sh, and export_result.sh. Consistent across all task files.
Web search confirms Mitosis sample is a 5D stack with Z (5 slices), Channels (2), Time (51 frames). https://imagej.net/plugins/image5d documents 5D image processing capabilities.
Web search confirms Mitosis sample has 2 channels (fluorescence and transmission). HeLa cells with H2B-GFP construct provide chromatin visualization in two channels.
Task description and multiple web sources consistently reference 'Mitosis (26MB, 5D stack)' as the official sample size.

pi_items.verified_value: Built-in Fiji sample - HeLa cells expressing H2B-GFP
pi_items.source: Fiji official documentation and https://imagej.net/plugins/samples confirm Mitosis is a standard built-in sample accessible via File > Open Samples > Mitosis. Data is from real microscopy (NIH source), not synthetic.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the official Fiji Mitosis sample dataset (26MB, 5D hyperstack) containing real HeLa cells expressing H2B-GFP chromatin marker. The dataset has confirmed dimensions: 5 Z-slices, 2 channels (fluorescence + transmission), and 51 time frames. The task expects the kymograph output to have height = 51 pixels (matching time frames) and minimum width = 20 pixels. Output files are saved to /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/: mitosis_projection.tif (intermediate Z-projection preserving time series) and mitosis_kymograph.tif (final resliced kymograph). The verifier checks for correct temporal dimension (height=51, not Z=5) and validates that the Z-projection step preserved all 51 time frames.
